judge_details.reasoning: The Sunday dialogue is functional but feels like a soft-focus epilogue rather than a scene with real tension or specificity. Characters are reflecting on the week in abstract terms ('honest work,' 'got out of our own way') rather than engaging with anything concrete about jalapeño corn dog bites, and the voices have converged into a single philosophical register that erases the distinctions built earlier in the week. Devon Park is absent despite being the one who pushed it live on Saturday, which is a missed continuity beat.
strengths: Callbacks to prior days' themes (restraint, honesty) provide some continuity
Marcus's exit line feels earned and character-consistent
issues: No recipe-specific language — could be about any project at any company
All five characters sound nearly identical in tone and vocabulary
No tension or friction; everyone agrees on everything
Devon Park's absence after Saturday's live push is a continuity gap
Steph's distinctive self-doubt voice from earlier in the week is completely absent
